data.text: Reading the escalation files before making changes.

data.output:
1	package escalation
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"time"
7	
8		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
9	)
10	
11	// SuccessStatus mirrors agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess. The agent package's
12	// TestEscalatableStatusesMatchAgentVocab guard catches drift if either side
13	// renames. Defined locally so escalation does not import agent.
14	const SuccessStatus = "success"
15	
16	// EscalatableStatuses is the set of executor status strings that warrant
17	// retrying with a higher tier. Mirrors a subset of agent.ExecuteBeadStatus*
18	// strings; the agent package has a TestEscalatableStatusesMatchAgentVocab
19	// guard (see agent/tier_escalation_alignment_test.go) to catch drift.
20	var EscalatableStatuses = map[string]bool{
21		"execution_failed":             true,
22		"post_run_check_failed":        true,
23		"land_conflict":                true,
24		"structural_validation_failed": true,
25	}
26	
27	// ShouldEscalate reports whether status warrants escalating to the next tier.
28	// Structural failures (e.g. validation errors) do not escalate because a
29	// smarter model cannot fix a malformed prompt or corrupted bead state.
30	func ShouldEscalate(status string) bool {
31		return EscalatableStatuses[status]
32	}
33	
34	// ProviderCooldownDuration is how long an unhealthy harness is skipped before
35	// re-probing. Five minutes gives most transient errors time to resolve without
36	// locking out a provider for too long.
37	//
38	// Per-harness cooldown tracking moved to the upstream agent service in v0.8.0:
39	// callers record failures via svc.RecordRouteAttempt and read cooldown state
40	// via svc.RouteStatus's RouteCandidateStatus.Healthy (ddx-7bc0c8d5).
41	const ProviderCooldownDuration = 5 * time.Minute
42	
43	// FormatTierAttemptBody formats the body of a tier-attempt bead event.
44	func FormatTierAttemptBody(tier, harness, model, probeResult, detail string) string {
45		body := fmt.Sprintf("tier=%s harness=%s model=%s", tier, harness, model)
46		if probeResult != "" {
47			body += " probe=" + probeResult
48		}
49		if detail != "" {
50			body += "\n" + detail
51		}
52		return body
53	}
54	
55	// EscalationWinningExhausted is the sentinel value written into the
56	// winning_tier field of an escalation-summary body when the escalation loop
57	// ran through every eligible tier without producing a successful attempt.
58	const EscalationWinningExhausted = "exhausted"
59	
60	// TierAttemptRecord is one row of an escalation trace. It captures the
61	// tier/harness/model that was tried, the status that attempt returned, and
62	// the cost/duration the harness reported for that attempt. Skipped tiers
63	// (no viable provider) are recorded with zero cost and zero duration.
64	type TierAttemptRecord struct {
65		Tier       string  `json:"tier"`
66		Harness    string  `json:"harness,omitempty"`
67		Model      string  `json:"model,omitempty"`
68		Status     string  `json:"status"`
69		CostUSD    float64 `json:"cost_usd"`
70		DurationMS int64   `json:"duration_ms"`
71	}
72	
73	// EscalationSummary is the structured body of a kind:escalation-summary bead
74	// event. It captures the entire escalation trace so an operator can diagnose
75	// which tiers were tried, which one won (if any), and how much the path cost.
76	type EscalationSummary struct {
77		TiersAttempted []TierAttemptRecord `json:"tiers_attempted"`
78		WinningTier    string              `json:"winning_tier"`
79		TotalCostUSD   float64             `json:"total_cost_usd"`
80		WastedCostUSD  float64             `json:"wasted_cost_usd"`
81	}
82	
83	// BuildEscalationSummary computes the summary body from the ordered list of
84	// attempts. winningTier is the string of the tier whose attempt succeeded;
85	// pass "" when the escalation was exhausted, in which case winning_tier is
86	// set to EscalationWinningExhausted. Total cost is the sum of all attempt
87	// costs; wasted cost is the sum of attempts whose status is not
88	// SuccessStatus.
89	func BuildEscalationSummary(attempts []TierAttemptRecord, winningTier string) EscalationSummary {
90		out := EscalationSummary{
91			TiersAttempted: append([]TierAttemptRecord{}, attempts...),
92			WinningTier:    winningTier,
93		}
94		if out.WinningTier == "" {
95			out.WinningTier = EscalationWinningExhausted
96		}
97		for _, a := range attempts {
98			out.TotalCostUSD += a.CostUSD
99			if a.Status != SuccessStatus {
100				out.WastedCostUSD += a.CostUSD
101			}
102		}
103		return out
104	}
105	
106	// BeadEventAppender records append-only evidence events on a bead. Mirrors
107	// agent.BeadEventAppender so escalation can append events without importing
108	// agent. *bead.Store satisfies both interfaces.
109	type BeadEventAppender interface {
110		AppendEvent(id string, event bead.BeadEvent) error
111	}
112	
113	// AppendEscalationSummaryEvent writes a kind:escalation-summary event to the
114	// bead with a JSON body describing the tier escalation trace. It is a
115	// best-effort operation — errors from the underlying store are returned so
116	// callers can log them, but callers typically ignore the error so telemetry
117	// failures never abort the main execute-bead flow.
118	func AppendEscalationSummaryEvent(appender BeadEventAppender, beadID, actor string, attempts []TierAttemptRecord, winningTier string, createdAt time.Time) error {
119		if appender == nil || beadID == "" {
120			return nil
121		}
122		summary := BuildEscalationSummary(attempts, winningTier)
123		body, err := json.Marshal(summary)
124		if err != nil {
125			return err
126		}
127		return appender.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
128			Kind:      "escalation-summary",
129			Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("winning_tier=%s attempts=%d total_cost_usd=%.4f wasted_cost_usd=%.4f", summary.WinningTier, len(attempts), summary.TotalCostUSD, summary.WastedCostUSD),
130			Body:      string(body),
131			Actor:     actor,
132			Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
133			CreatedAt: createdAt,
134		})
135	}
136

cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go
cli/internal/escalation/escalation_test.go
cli/internal/escalation/execution_hint.go
cli/internal/escalation/infrastructure.go

1	package escalation
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"testing"
6		"time"
7	
8		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
9		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
10		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
11	)
12	
13	// --- Local mirrors of agent.ExecuteBeadStatus* so these tests do not import
14	// the agent package. Their string values must stay in sync with
15	// cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go; the agent package's
16	// TestEscalatableStatusesMatchAgentVocab guards against drift for the
17	// statuses that drive escalation.
18	const (
19		statusExecutionFailed            = "execution_failed"
20		statusNoChanges                  = "no_changes"
21		statusPostRunCheckFailed         = "post_run_check_failed"
22		statusLandConflict               = "land_conflict"
23		statusStructuralValidationFailed = "structural_validation_failed"
24		statusAlreadySatisfied           = "already_satisfied"
25		statusSuccess                    = "success"
26	)
27	
28	// --- EscalationSummary ---
29	
30	// recordingAppender captures events appended by AppendEscalationSummaryEvent
31	// so tests can assert on the kind, summary, and JSON body.
32	type recordingAppender struct {
33		events []struct {
34			id    string
35			event bead.BeadEvent
36		}
37	}
38	
39	func (r *recordingAppender) AppendEvent(id string, event bead.BeadEvent) error {
40		r.events = append(r.events, struct {
41			id    string
42			event bead.BeadEvent
43		}{id: id, event: event})
44		return nil
45	}
46	
47	// TestEscalationSummary exercises the 3-tier escalation scenario from the
48	// bead: cheap fails, standard fails, smart succeeds. The emitted
49	// kind:escalation-summary event body must contain all three tier records
50	// with correct statuses, costs, and a winning_tier/wasted_cost_usd roll-up.
51	func TestEscalationSummary(t *testing.T) {
52		attempts := []TierAttemptRecord{
53			{Tier: "cheap", Harness: "agent", Model: "cheap-model", Status: statusExecutionFailed, CostUSD: 0.02, DurationMS: 1200},
54			{Tier: "standard", Harness: "codex", Model: "standard-model", Status: statusNoChanges, CostUSD: 0.15, DurationMS: 3400},
55			{Tier: "smart", Harness: "claude", Model: "smart-model", Status: statusSuccess, CostUSD: 0.80, DurationMS: 9000},
56		}
57	
58		summary := BuildEscalationSummary(attempts, "smart")
59		require.Equal(t, "smart", summary.WinningTier)
60		require.Len(t, summary.TiersAttempted, 3)
61		assert.InDelta(t, 0.97, summary.TotalCostUSD, 1e-9)
62		assert.InDelta(t, 0.17, summary.WastedCostUSD, 1e-9, "cheap (0.02) + standard (0.15) wasted, smart succeeded")
63		assert.Equal(t, "cheap", summary.TiersAttempted[0].Tier)
64		assert.Equal(t, statusExecutionFailed, summary.TiersAttempted[0].Status)
65		assert.Equal(t, statusNoChanges, summary.TiersAttempted[1].Status)
66		assert.Equal(t, statusSuccess, summary.TiersAttempted[2].Status)
67	
68		// Now wire through AppendEscalationSummaryEvent and verify the emitted
69		// event has kind:escalation-summary, a summary line, and a JSON body
70		// that round-trips to the same EscalationSummary.
71		appender := &recordingAppender{}
72		err := AppendEscalationSummaryEvent(appender, "ddx-test-1", "test-actor", attempts, "smart", time.Unix(1, 0).UTC())
73		require.NoError(t, err)
74		require.Len(t, appender.events, 1)
75	
76		ev := appender.events[0]
77		assert.Equal(t, "ddx-test-1", ev.id)
78		assert.Equal(t, "escalation-summary", ev.event.Kind)
79		assert.Equal(t, "test-actor", ev.event.Actor)
80		assert.Contains(t, ev.event.Summary, "winning_tier=smart")
81		assert.Contains(t, ev.event.Summary, "attempts=3")
82	
83		var decoded EscalationSummary
84		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(ev.event.Body), &decoded))
85		assert.Equal(t, "smart", decoded.WinningTier)
86		require.Len(t, decoded.TiersAttempted, 3)
87		assert.Equal(t, "cheap", decoded.TiersAttempted[0].Tier)
88		assert.Equal(t, "agent", decoded.TiersAttempted[0].Harness)
89		assert.Equal(t, "cheap-model", decoded.TiersAttempted[0].Model)
90		assert.Equal(t, statusExecutionFailed, decoded.TiersAttempted[0].Status)
91		assert.InDelta(t, 0.02, decoded.TiersAttempted[0].CostUSD, 1e-9)
92		assert.Equal(t, int64(1200), decoded.TiersAttempted[0].DurationMS)
93		assert.InDelta(t, 0.97, decoded.TotalCostUSD, 1e-9)
94		assert.InDelta(t, 0.17, decoded.WastedCostUSD, 1e-9)
95	}
96	
97	// TestEscalationSummaryExhausted verifies the exhausted case (no tier
98	// succeeded): winning_tier is "exhausted" and all costs are wasted.
99	func TestEscalationSummaryExhausted(t *testing.T) {
100		attempts := []TierAttemptRecord{
101			{Tier: "cheap", Harness: "agent", Model: "c", Status: statusExecutionFailed, CostUSD: 0.01, DurationMS: 900},
102			{Tier: "smart", Harness: "claude", Model: "s", Status: statusExecutionFailed, CostUSD: 0.50, DurationMS: 7000},
103		}
104		summary := BuildEscalationSummary(attempts, "")
105		assert.Equal(t, EscalationWinningExhausted, summary.WinningTier)
106		assert.InDelta(t, 0.51, summary.TotalCostUSD, 1e-9)
107		assert.InDelta(t, 0.51, summary.WastedCostUSD, 1e-9, "every attempt wasted when no tier succeeded")
108	}
109	
110	// TestEscalationSummaryBuildSourceSliceIndependent verifies the attempts
111	// slice in the returned summary is independent of the caller's slice —
112	// mutating the caller's slice must not change the summary.
113	func TestEscalationSummaryBuildSourceSliceIndependent(t *testing.T) {
114		attempts := []TierAttemptRecord{
115			{Tier: "cheap", Status: statusSuccess, CostUSD: 0.05},
116		}
117		summary := BuildEscalationSummary(attempts, "cheap")
118		attempts[0].Tier = "mutated"
119		assert.Equal(t, "cheap", summary.TiersAttempted[0].Tier, "summary must hold an independent copy of attempts")
120	}
121	
122	// --- ShouldEscalate ---
123	
124	func TestShouldEscalateExecutionFailed(t *testing.T) {
125		assert.True(t, ShouldEscalate(statusExecutionFailed))
126	}
127	
128	func TestShouldEscalateNoChangesIsFalse(t *testing.T) {
129		assert.False(t, ShouldEscalate(statusNoChanges))
130	}
131	
132	func TestShouldEscalatePostRunCheckFailed(t *testing.T) {
133		assert.True(t, ShouldEscalate(statusPostRunCheckFailed))
134	}
135	
136	func TestShouldEscalateLandConflict(t *testing.T) {
137		assert.True(t, ShouldEscalate(statusLandConflict))
138	}
139	
140	func TestShouldEscalateSuccessIsFalse(t *testing.T) {
141		assert.False(t, ShouldEscalate(statusSuccess))
142	}
143	
144	func TestShouldEscalateStructuralValidationIsTrue(t *testing.T) {
145		assert.True(t, ShouldEscalate(statusStructuralValidationFailed))
146	}
147	
148	func TestShouldEscalateAlreadySatisfiedIsFalse(t *testing.T) {
149		assert.False(t, ShouldEscalate(statusAlreadySatisfied))
150	}
151	
152	// --- FormatTierAttemptBody ---
153	
154	func TestFormatTierAttemptBodyWithAllFields(t *testing.T) {
155		body := FormatTierAttemptBody("cheap", "claude", "claude-haiku-4-5", "ok", "execution failed")
156		assert.Contains(t, body, "tier=cheap")
157		assert.Contains(t, body, "harness=claude")
158		assert.Contains(t, body, "model=claude-haiku-4-5")
159		assert.Contains(t, body, "probe=ok")
160		assert.Contains(t, body, "execution failed")
161	}
162	
163	func TestFormatTierAttemptBodyNoProbeNoDetail(t *testing.T) {
164		body := FormatTierAttemptBody("standard", "codex", "gpt-5.4", "", "")
165		assert.Contains(t, body, "tier=standard")
166		assert.NotContains(t, body, "probe=")
167	}
168

2. Replace the old `True` test with `TestStructuralValidationFailed_DoesNotEscalate` asserting `False`
3. Remove it from the alignment test's expected map

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(escalation): remove structural_validation_failed from EscalatableStatuses [ddx-8c9adb54]

Structural failures cannot be fixed by a smarter model, so escalating
them wastes budget. Remove the status from EscalatableStatuses, update
the alignment test expected set, and flip the existing test to assert
false; add TestStructuralValidationFailed_DoesNotEscalate per AC.
EOF
)"

data.text:
Done. All four ACs satisfied:

1. `escalation.go` `EscalatableStatuses` no longer contains `"structural_validation_failed"`
2. `TestStructuralValidationFailed_DoesNotEscalate` asserts `ShouldEscalate` returns `false`
3. `go test ./internal/escalation/... ./internal/agent/...` green
4. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes
